questions.question: What happens when you have used all your complimentary AI responses?

options.text: Notion AI will automatically upgrade you to the Business plan.
options.explanation: Notion AI will not upgrade you automatically. You must manually upgrade your plan.

options.text: You will see a message that an upgrade to the Business or Enterprise Plan is necessary.
options.explanation: The article states that once you've used all complimentary responses, a message will appear requesting an upgrade to Business or Enterprise.

options.text: You will be locked out of the Notion application entirely.
options.explanation: You will not be locked out of the app; only the AI features will be restricted until you upgrade.

options.text: Your remaining responses will double to compensate.
options.explanation: There is no compensation or doubling of responses; the limit is fixed.

questions.question: Who is required to be a workspace owner to upgrade the plan for AI features?

options.text: Any member of the workspace.
options.explanation: The article specifically requires the user to be a 'workspace owner' to perform the upgrade.

options.text: A workspace owner.
options.explanation: The text states: 'You’ll need to be a workspace owner'.

options.text: The administrator of the billing system.
options.explanation: There is no mention of a billing system administrator; the requirement is for a workspace owner.

options.text: A super admin.
options.explanation: The specific role required is 'workspace owner', not 'super admin'.

questions.question: Where must you be located (platform) to upgrade your plan to access AI features?

options.text: On mobile devices.
options.explanation: The article specifies that the upgrade must be done 'on desktop'.

options.text: On desktop.
options.explanation: The text explicitly says: 'and on desktop, to upgrade your plan'.

options.text: On the web interface.
options.explanation: While web is similar to desktop, the specific requirement mentioned is 'desktop'.

options.text: Only in the Notion Mail app.
options.explanation: The article mentions Notion Mail as a place where AI is used, but the upgrade link is for the general plan upgrade.

questions.question: Does adding an autofill property count toward your complimentary AI responses?

options.text: No, it is free forever.
options.explanation: Adding an autofill property is listed as an AI action that counts as a response.

options.text: Yes, it counts as an AI response.
options.explanation: The article lists 'adding an autofill property' as one of the actions that counts toward complimentary responses.

options.text: Only if the property is a date type.
options.explanation: The text does not differentiate by property type; any autofill action counts.

options.text: No, it is not considered an AI action.
options.explanation: The article explicitly defines 'using AI blocks, adding an autofill property' as actions that count.

questions.question: Does using Notion AI within Notion Mail count toward your complimentary AI responses?

options.text: No, email is excluded from the count.
options.explanation: The article states that responses received from Notion AI in Notion Mail count as responses.

options.text: Yes, it counts as an AI response.
options.explanation: The text states: 'in Notion or Notion Mail and receive a response from Notion AI, that counts as an AI response'.

options.text: It counts as half a response.
options.explanation: There is no concept of partial responses; it counts fully as a response.

options.text: Only if you are a Business plan user.
options.explanation: The count applies regardless of plan status for the action itself.

questions.question: If you click 'Try again' on an AI response, how does it affect your complimentary response count?

options.text: It does not change the count.
options.explanation: The article states that clicking 'Try again' counts as an *additional* AI response.

options.text: It uses a different currency or token system.
options.explanation: There is no separate token system; every 'Try again' uses a standard complimentary response.

options.text: It counts as an additional AI response.
options.explanation: The text explicitly says: 'Anytime you click Try again, that counts as an additional AI response'.

options.text: It adds 1.5 times the response to your total.
options.explanation: There is no multiplier; it adds exactly one additional response.

questions.question: Which of the following specific actions is explicitly exempted from counting toward complimentary responses?

options.text: Creating a database with Notion AI.
options.explanation: The article explicitly lists this as the 'only exception' to the rule that actions count as responses.

options.text: Editing existing content.
options.explanation: Editing existing content is listed as an action that counts as a response.

options.text: Asking a question.
options.explanation: Asking a question is listed as an action that counts as a response.

options.text: Creating new content.
options.explanation: Creating new content is listed as an action that counts as a response.

questions.question: Which plans allow you to continue using AI features after exhausting complimentary responses?

options.text: Personal plan.
options.explanation: The article specifies that you must upgrade to the 'Business or Enterprise Plan'.

options.text: Business or Enterprise Plan.
options.explanation: The text states that an upgrade to the Business or Enterprise Plan is necessary to continue using AI features.

options.text: Team plan.
options.explanation: There is no mention of a 'Team' plan; the options are Business and Enterprise.

options.text: Free plan.
options.explanation: The free plan only offers complimentary responses; continuing requires an upgrade.

questions.question: What is the criteria for an action to count as an AI response?

options.text: You must take an AI action and receive a response from Notion AI.
options.explanation: The article defines a response as taking an AI action (e.g., editing, asking) and receiving a result.

options.text: You must create content using Notion AI blocks.
options.explanation: While using blocks is one way to take an action, the definition is broader (includes questions, autofill, etc.).

options.text: You must receive a response from Notion AI.
options.explanation: You must take an action *and* receive a response; receiving a response without taking an action isn't possible.

options.text: You must type a specific keyword to trigger the AI.
options.explanation: The criteria are based on actions taken and responses received, not specific keywords.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT included in the list of actions that count as an AI response?

options.text: Creating new content.
options.explanation: Creating new content is explicitly listed as an action that counts.

options.text: Asking a question.
options.explanation: Asking a question is explicitly listed as an action that counts.

options.text: Using Notion Mail.
options.explanation: Using Notion Mail is explicitly listed as a platform where actions count.

options.text: Viewing the Notion AI chat history.
options.explanation: Viewing chat history is not an action that generates a new response; it is passive.
